scope: false-positive audit for discoverRefsFromValue / extractRefsFromText / canonicalizeReference
likely_true_positive_sources: json/text content containing nested IPFS references
likely_false_positive_sources: generic href fields in HTML or error payloads
metadata fields that mention links in prose
custom object keys not meant as reference carriers
risk_notes: discoverRefsFromValue currently walks many object branches and only skips the explicit key names it reads directly; this can still capture refs buried in unrelated text values.
extractRefsFromText is regex-based and may match CID-like values in arbitrary prose.
The current maxRefs/maxNodes limits reduce runaway scans but do not prevent semantic noise.
The current implementation is intentionally permissive and therefore better suited to a broad discovery phase than to strict validation.
recommendations: separate permissive discovery from any future strict validation layer
introduce a clearer whitelist for reference-bearing keys before making discovery stricter
avoid using error/metadata/prose objects as authoritative reference sources
keep the current behavior unchanged for now; document it first
alignment_with_conclusion: do not hard-reset rootCid semantics yet
do not tighten CAR validation in this phase
